ccel.title: NPNF1-03. On the Holy Trinity; Doctrinal Treatises; Moral Treatises

title: Section 2

source.title: NPNF1-03. On the Holy Trinity; Doctrinal Treatises; Moral Treatises

verses.book: On Care to Be Had for the Dead.
verses.text: But this being the case, how to this opinion that should not be contrary which the Apostle says, “For we shall all stand before the judgment-seat of Christ, that each may receive according to the things he hath done by the body, whether good or bad;” this, thou signifiest, thou dost not well see.

verses.book: On Care to Be Had for the Dead.
verses.text: For this apostolic sentence doth before death admonish to be done, that which may profit after death; not then, first, when there is to be now a receiving of that which a person shall have done before death.

verses.book: On Care to Be Had for the Dead.
verses.text: True, but this question is thus solved, namely, that there is a certain kind of life by which is acquired, while one lives in this body, that it should be possible for these things to be of some help to the departed; and, consequently, it is “according to the things done by the body,” that they are aided by the things which shall, after they have left the body, be religiously done on their behalf.

verses.book: On Care to Be Had for the Dead.
verses.text: For there are whom these things aid nothing at all, namely, when they are done either for persons whose merits are so evil, that neither by such things are they worthy to be aided; or for persons whose merits are so good, that of such things they have no need as aids.

verses.book: On Care to Be Had for the Dead.
verses.text: Of the kind of life, therefore, which each hath led by the body, doth it come, that these things profit or profit not, whatever are piously done on his behalf when he has left the body.

verses.book: On Care to Be Had for the Dead.
verses.text: For touching merit whereby these things profit, if none have been gotten in this life, it is in vain sought after this life.

verses.book: On Care to Be Had for the Dead.
verses.text: So it comes to pass as well that not unmeaningly doth the Church, or care of friends, bestow upon the departed whatever of religion it shall be able; as also that, nevertheless, each receiveth “according to the things which he hath done by the body, whether it be good or bad,” the Lord rendering unto each according to his works.

verses.book: On Care to Be Had for the Dead.
verses.text: For, that this which is bestowed should be capable of profiting him after the body, this was acquired in that life which he hath led in the body.
